headd: Objective Test Questions: Answer all the questions

S1.headd: In each of the following sentences, there is one word or group of words unde dined and one gap. From the list of words or group of words lettered A to D, choose the one that is most nearly opposite in meaning to the underlined word or group of words and that will, at the same time correctly fill the gap in the sentence;

S1.qs: 1.	Angela is a very **indolent** but her sister Mary is one of the most ................... girls in the school.
2.	Rather than **support** the Chairman, the members of his board expressed ................. to his plans.
3.	In any group there are people who display **apathy** and others who show a lot of ................
4.	Femi was very ................ about his political ambition but his sister was **open** about it.
5.	Ngozi's beauty is **natural** but her sister’s is rather ..........................
6.	The testimony of the witness was so **vague** that it was difficult to learn the .................... details of the case
7.	It is amazing how Jide became so **proud**, his parents have always been ......................
8.	The workshop was intended to .............. the workers who had been **confused** by management’s decisions
9.	Johnson is consistently **assertive** but his twin brother is rather ...................

S2.headd: From the words lettered A to D, choose the word that best completes each of the following sentences:

S2.qs: 10.	The country’s aviation industry has lost at least four ..................... this year alone.
11.	My cousin has been married for fourteen years but he has no .............
12.	Corruption is one of the ills in this country which must be ...................
13.	The boys covered their noses because of the ................. odour in the area
14.	It is .................. on parents to advise their children against keeping bad company
15.	The man said that he would no longer ................. his stepson because of his bad behaviour
16.	The accused was charged with ................. because he caused his brother’s death.
17.	The judge acquitted Musa because he was a first ...................
18.	He rendered ............... assistance to the community
19.	The team manager looked .................... when his boys lost the match

S3.headd: After each of the following sentences, a list of possible interpretations is given. Choose the interpretation that you consider most appropriate for each sentence.

S3.qs: 20.	We left Kaduna one year ago to the day. This means that we left Kaduna

opts.q1: B. one year minus a day
opts.q2: C. one year and a day

S3.qs: 21.	Tamuno’s statement is true to a degree. This means that his statement is
22.	Both the doves and the hawks agreed on this issue. This means that there is agreement by both

opts.q0: A. those who prefer peace and those who want the use of force
opts.q1: B. the poor and the rich
opts.q2: C. the government side and the opposition
opts.q3: D. those supporting changes and those against them

S3.qs: 23.	Japan has blazed a trail in the production of electronics. This means that in the production of electronics, Japan
24.	Mohammed Ali won the fight hands down. This means that Mohammed Ali won

opts.q0: A. after a very tough fight

S3.qs: 25.	The scientist has broken new  ground in his research. This means that the scientist has

opts.q1: B. taken up a new interest
opts.q3: D. offered a new explanation for his discovery

S3.qs: 26.	The chief decided to obtain the land by hook or by crook. This means that the chief decided to get the land
27.	The lady swallowed the whole story nook, line and sinker. This means that the lady

opts.q2: C. had her doubts about the story

S3.qs: 28.	He went too far by insulting the Director’s wife. This means that

opts.q0: A. he went out with the Director’s wife and insulted her
opts.q1: B. he walked too long for the Director’s wife
opts.q2: C. he was too close to the Director’s wife
opts.q3: D. he should not have gone to the extent of insulting the Director’s wife

S3.qs: 29.	The decision to dismiss the night watchmen was taken in my absence. This means that the decision was

opts.q0: A. taken as I was leaving
opts.q3: D. not disclosed to the night watchman

S4.headd: From the words or group of words lettered A to D below each of the following sentences, choose the word or group of words that is nearest in meaning to the underlined word or group of words as it is used in the sentence

S4.qs: 30.	You have failed to **heed** the warning about your marriage
31.	The scholarship committee has **endorsed** the student’s bursary
32.	All my efforts to settle the quarrel between the two friends were **futile**
33.	My uncle made the right choice of career as a detective because he is a very **observant** man
34.	I cannot continue the argument because you are **biased**
35.	Guidance counselors should regard all the information given by students as **confidential**.
36.	It is advisable to buy **durable** clothes nowadays
37.	There are various **options** open to you on this matter.
38.	To drive on through the red light is an **infringement** of the law

S6.headd: In the following passages, the numbered gaps indicate missing words. Against each number in the list below each passage, four options are offered in columns lettered A to D, choose the word that is the most suitable to fill the numbered gap in the passage.
PB: Communication is a way of sharing information, ideas or jokes with somebody by talking, writing or using signs. Without communication, no one can learn or teach.

The various means of --89-- communication includes letter writing, telephone, telex, fax and so on. The telephone is one of the --90-- means of communication without the risk of travelling. One can make a telephone call from a home or a public telephone --91--.

Telephones are connected to each other by wires that transmit --92-- by electric --93--. The type of telephone common to people is the --94-- telephone. There are local and international --95--. To telephone someone, you have to --96-- a number.

A ringing --97-- indicates that the line is through. As soon as the receiver is lifted at the other end, the ringing stops and communication begins. The communication is --90-- as soon as the receiver is put back on its cradle. Telephone --99-- usually pay their --100-- through specified banks.
